response:
"the relationship between constants a and b is derived from substituting x equals two into the polynomial equation to yield zero.
specifically substituting two yields sixteen minus twelve plus two a plus b equals zero so b equals negative four minus two a.
to ensure x equals two is a double root the derivative of the polynomial must also equal zero at that specific point.
the derivative function is six x squared minus six x plus a and setting x to two gives twenty four minus twelve plus a equals zero.
solving this linear equation reveals that a must equal negative twelve to satisfy the condition for a repeated root.
substituting a equals negative twelve back into the relationship equation yields b equals negative four minus two times negative twelve.
calculating the final value shows that b equals twenty to complete the set of encryption keys for the game.
the final values for the constants are a equals negative twelve and b equals twenty ensuring the double root at two."
